data.title: New Padel Courts and Weekend Programming with Certified Integrated Health Coach, Nora Tobin, Usher in New Wave of Wellness at The Ocean Club, A Four Seasons Resort, Bahamas
data.description:
New Padel Courts and Weekend Programming with Certified Integrated Health Coach, Nora Tobin, Usher in New Wave of Wellness at The Ocean Club, A Four Seasons Resort, Bahamas

New Padel Courts and Weekend Programming with Certified Integrated Health Coach, Nora Tobin, Usher in New Wave of Wellness at The Ocean Club, A Four Seasons Resort, Bahamas

PR Newswire

PARADISE ISLAND, Bahamas, April 7, 2026

The Ocean Club's expanded racquet sports offerings, premium on-site fitness offerings and special workshops with Nora Tobin ensure guests can experience a truly rejuvenating getaway

PARADISE ISLAND, Bahamas, April 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Ocean Club, A Four Seasons Resort, Bahamas is serving up the latest trend in racquet sports, with the arrival of two new padel courts. The padel courts, illuminated for nighttime play, accompany three Har-Tru tennis courts, all newly resurfaced for a premium court experience. Located steps away from the gorgeous, tiered Versailles Gardens and picturesque waterfall lagoon pool, the courts are easily walkable from the main resort guestroom wings.

In addition, alongside the padel courts, guests can enjoy a new sport court with basketball hoop, and an adjacent turf field ideal for soccer and other lawn games. The enhancements further position The Ocean Club as a premium destination for those who prioritize integrating physical fitness into their vacation plans. With instruction available by professional Marvin Rolle, guests who are experts in padel or newly beginning will find customized coaching available, tailored for their level of play. Rolle is an esteemed player who has represented The Bahamas in multiple competitions. Rolle won a bronze medal in the 2010 Central American and Caribbean Games. His father, Leo Rolle, was a distinguished tennis professional at The Ocean Club for over 50 years, so the love of tennis runs in the family.

"The Ocean Club offers many activities for guests looking to have fun and stay active as part of their vacation," says John Conway, General Manager. "Padel courts are a wonderful enhancement to our beautiful tennis courts, which are surrounded by incredible palm trees for a tropical experience when on the courts. With our water sports, fitness center and outdoor jungle gym, bicycle rentals, plus access to the gorgeous Ocean Club Golf Course, there are many options for our guests to stay fit while they're vacationing in our slice of paradise."

The Padel and sport courts will be available to guests this May.

Nora Tobin Wellness Weekends
The Ocean Club also invites guests to maximize their wellness experience on property by offering a series of immersive Rejuvenation Weekends with special guest Nora Tobin, a certified integrated health coach, trusted wellness strategist to Fortune 100 leaders, and founder of a sustainable wellness company. Grounded in her five performance pillars: stress optimization, reparative sleep, performance, advanced nutrition, and biophilic design, guests may choose a fully immersive retreat or a selection of highly tailored à la carte sessions. Starting this June, Tobin's fitness classes and workshops are offered complimentary to registered overnight guests of the resort, and guests can partake in a full weekend of classes and coaching with Tobin or select from several individual classes and workshops.

Tobin will lead four different sessions at the Resort, featuring fitness classes, guided meditation, beachside yoga, and more. Set within The Ocean Club's iconic gardens and along its pristine shoreline, each experience is designed to recalibrate energy, restore balance, and elevate performance. Signature offerings include Vino & Vinyasa, a sophisticated fusion of movement and indulgence, alongside performance-driven sessions such as Versailles Athletic Club and Oceanic Healing, where guests engage with the restorative cognitive effects of the ocean environment. The Breathwork Lab and Sunset Circadian Reset introduce practical tools top performers use to build resilience, clarity, and sustained vitality.

"The Ocean Club creates an immediate physiological shift; the oceanic environment naturally restores clarity and energy," says Tobin. "Add a passion fruit martini at sunset, and you have a version of wellness that feels as indulgent as it is effective."

The Wellness Weekends with Nora Tobin will be offered on the following dates, with central themes for each retreat:

June 5-8, 2026"Longevity Lab"
July 9-12, 2026 "Fit Family"
October 16-19 "Nature Reset"
December 4-7, 2026 "Holiday Healthy"

Tobin's array of classes are offered from sunrise to sunset, enabling guests to choose a time that best fits their schedule. The first activation, June 5-8, will include a sleep ritual session, an agility drills session, a somatic sound and complexion reset, oceanic healing meditation, circadian sunrise experience, fountain of youth fitness class, and regenerative yoga. The second activation, July 9-12, will include classes such as the family reset ritual, train like a tennis pro, foundational strength, fueling the modern family, intro to padel, family yoga and raising healthy performers.

Guests who have Resort room reservations over the dates of Tobin's sessions are invited to participate in as many of the classes they would like. Some classes will have space limitations, and signups are available on a first come, first serve basis. Guests can register directly through the Fitness Centre. Blending intellectual rigor with effortless luxury, these weekends redefine modern wellness, where rejuvenation is both intentional and indulgent. Under Tobin's guidance, each experience delivers immediate impact and a refined, lasting approach to sustainable performance and longevity.

Fitness and Activities
The Resort's Fitness Centre offers an indoor gym plus an outdoor "jungle gym," that is a hit among guests for its tropical setting and unique way to work out. While many hotel gyms are often found in the lower floors, frequently without even windows, working out amongst the palms in the balmy climate is a true island experience. With regular fitness classes from cardio to Pilates offered multiple times per week, there is something for everyone to enjoy. Nearby, the 18-hole Tom Weiskopf designed Ocean Club Golf Course presents some of the most gorgeous scenery, along with a challenging layout. Additionally, water sports abound, with kayaking, snorkelling and more all available to be arranged through the Concierge.

In between fitness classes, swimming, tennis, padel and golf, at The Ocean Club, the main activity is relaxation—it is the place to truly experience being on "island time" and taking a break. Unwind on the Resort's many hammocks strung between the palms, take a float on the beach or in the quiet adult-only Versailles pool, newly enhanced with gorgeous cherry red parasol umbrellas and coordinating chaise lounges, with a custom tropical sea life pattern. After play time on the sport court, take a nap to the soothing sounds of the waterfall found at the family Lagoon Pool. Indulge in a spa treatment at the Resort's Balinese-style spa, featuring eight intimate private spa bungalows with deep soaking tubs and indoor and outdoor space to unwind.

Reserve a Wellness-Minded Escape
The Ocean Club offers numerous room types, from guestrooms and suites that can outer-connect, as well as two garden bungalows that have private plunge pools, and three stunning three-and-four-bedroom beachfront private villa residences. The guestrooms at the Hartford Wing are centered by a beautiful outdoor courtyard with fountain, while some have views of the beach, and others have views of a tropical garden. The guestrooms and suites in the Crescent Wing feature a beach-inspired color scheme in shades of sand and aqua. These spacious rooms all boast sweeping views of the Atlantic Ocean as well as a verdant green palm garden, dotted with hammocks and Adirondack chairs. The beach villa residences are the ultimate retreat for families or friends travelling together, featuring a full kitchen, a spacious outdoor terrace and infinity pool overlooking the ocean, and private access to the beach.

The Ocean Club, Four Seasons Residences Bahamas are now offering ownership, with anticipated completion in 2028. Guests can stop by the residential sales center in the Hartford Gallery, located in the Hartford Wing near The Spa entry, to learn more about this exciting new development.

Easy Access with Direct Flights to Nassau
It's easy to arrive to paradise, with 50 airports worldwide offering a direct flight to Nassau. Guests can reserve packages such as the Tennis in Paradise offer, to take advantage of unlimited court time, a lesson for two, and daily breakfast included, or the Return to Paradise offer, which includes a fourth night complimentary plus roundtrip private car transfers to and from the airport. For Resort reservations, contact 1-855-524-3152 or visit www.fourseasons.com/oceanclub/offers.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/new-padel-courts-and-weekend-programming-with-certified-integrated-health-coach-nora-tobin-usher-in-new-wave-of-wellness-at-the-ocean-club-a-four-seasons-resort-bahamas-302735849.html

SOURCE The Ocean Club, A Four Seasons Resort, Bahamas

data.title: Association of Progressive Rental Organizations Becomes Association of Professional Rental Organizations, Highlighting 46 Years of Leadership
data.description:
Association of Progressive Rental Organizations Becomes Association of Professional Rental Organizations, Highlighting 46 Years of Leadership

Association of Progressive Rental Organizations Becomes Association of Professional Rental Organizations, Highlighting 46 Years of Leadership

PR Newswire

AUSTIN, Texas, April 7, 2026

New Name Reflects Its Commitment to Advancing Standards and Strengthening Trust in the Rent-to-Own Industry

AUSTIN, Texas, April 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Founded in 1980, the Association of Progressive Rental Organizations (APRO) was created to give a voice to a misunderstood industry. Today, the organization has adopted a new name, the Association of Professional Rental Organizations (APRO), reflecting its decades-long evolution from legislative advocacy to the leader in advancing professionalism, education, and ethical standards across the rent-to-own (RTO) industry.

As the industry has matured, APRO has expanded its focus beyond lobbying and legislation to deliver tools, resources, and thought leadership that elevate professionalism and support member success. Key initiatives include:  

  • The release of The Rent-to-Own Revolution: The Definitive History of Advocacy and Consumer Access, co-authored by APRO CEO Charles Smitherman, offers a comprehensive look at the industry's evolution while reinforcing its role as a flexible, regulated model that expands consumer access.
  • Earlier this year, APRO announced the 50-State Lease Agreement Library, providing compliant, up-to-date lease agreements that enhance clarity and consumer protection across all 50 states.
  • APRO's Code of Ethics guides members in maintaining transparency, accountability, and consumer-first practices, which strengthens professional standards across the industry.
  • The creation of the 47-State Model Lease-Purchase Statutory Framework established a consistent regulatory foundation for the rent-to-own industry.

APRO's 2025 Industry Health Survey shows the U.S. RTO industry is strong and growing. In 2025, it generated $11.5 billion in revenue, serving 4.63 million households (about 1 in 29 U.S. households), and employed more than 42,000 full-time workers. Survey results also show that 89% of APRO members are optimistic about the future, with 39% very optimistic.

"The word 'Professional' communicates exactly who we are and where we're going as an industry," APRO CEO Charles Smitherman explained. "APRO members deliver a vital consumer option for millions of Americans, offering flexibility and responsible access to essential goods, while upholding the highest standards of service and ethics."

The rebrand is not only a reflection of APRO's history and growth, but also a promise of continued leadership and professionalism. Members have responded positively to the name change, viewing it as both a validation of their commitment and a call to further professionalize and modernize the industry.

Looking ahead, APRO plans to roll out new professional development programs, including a trust mark for dealers and certification courses for employees, designed to further strengthen credibility and consumer trust.

"From our conversations with RTO employees and customers across the U.S., we see firsthand the impact our members have on people's lives," Smitherman said. "Whether it's a single parent juggling work and family, or a college student getting the essential items they need, our mission is to provide access with dignity. APRO is committed to innovation and continuous improvement, ensuring every customer can rely on our members for flexible, trustworthy solutions - no matter what life brings."

Understanding Rent-to-Own: How the Industry Protects Consumers

APRO CEO Charles Smitherman explains the core principles of the rent-to-own (RTO) model and how the industry ensures consumer protection:

  • No Debt/No Obligation (Not a Loan): Rent-to-own is a rental transaction, not a credit-based loan. There is no debt, no interest, and no long-term obligation, allowing customers to stop at any time.
  • Flexible & Choice-Based: Customers have the flexibility to choose how they acquire items, with the options to return, reinstate, or renew at their own pace.
  • Essential Access & Dignity: The model provides immediate access to necessary household goods (like appliances and electronics) without requiring traditional credit, providing dignified, immediate ownership paths.
  • Regulated & Transparent: The industry is highly regulated, operating under specific, clear statutes in 47 states that require transparent, mandatory disclosures. 

To learn more about APRO, visit www.rtohq.org or follow the organization on Facebook and LinkedIn.

About APRO

The Association of Professional Rental Organizations (APRO), formerly Association of Progressive Rental Organizations, is the only national trade association representing the rent-to-own (RTO) industry. Established in 1980 to advocate for and support the interests of RTO businesses, APRO provides its 325 member brands with essential resources, industry insights, and a strong community network. Through educational initiatives, legislative advocacy, and professional development opportunities, APRO strives to elevate the standards and success of the RTO sector, ensuring it continues to thrive and serve customers with integrity and excellence.

Media Contact: Ethan Gutstein, Fishman Public Relations, (847) 945-1300 or egutstein@fishmanpr.com

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/association-of-progressive-rental-organizations-becomes-association-of-professional-rental-organizations-highlighting-46-years-of-leadership-302735671.html

SOURCE Association of Professional Rental Organizations (APRO)

data.title: For sale: One of Texas' most over-the-top ranches, with decadent main home, sports building, event venue, horse barn, lakeside cabin and field with nothing but sunflowers
data.description:
For sale: One of Texas' most over-the-top ranches, with decadent main home, sports building, event venue, horse barn, lakeside cabin and field with nothing but sunflowers

For sale: One of Texas' most over-the-top ranches, with decadent main home, sports building, event venue, horse barn, lakeside cabin and field with nothing but sunflowers

PR Newswire

DALLAS, April 7, 2026

DALLAS, April 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The award-winning TT Ranch Group at Briggs Freeman Sotheby's International Realty is proud to offer one of the most impressive properties in Texas — Pearl Ranch, a private estate in Maypearl, just 40 minutes south of the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. At 662+/- acres, it blends exceptionally refined luxury living with the rugged beauty of the Texas countryside.

Pearl Ranch is truly unrivaled, encompassing multiple residences, an entertainment pavilion, recreation center, event venue, fishing cabin and equestrian facilities. The setting is equally magnificent, offering a diverse habitat that transitions between expansive improved pastures and densely wooded areas, plus several lakes and ponds, a sunflower field, dramatic viewpoints and a sense of grandeur that is exceedingly rare this close to the nation's fastest-growing metroplex.

The crown jewel is the custom-built, stone main home with five bedrooms, chef's kitchen, wine cellar, cigar room, safe room and spectacular living spaces. Its quality craftsmanship includes massive architectural ceiling trusses and a foundation of slab, steel and piers — drilled 40 inches deep — built to last generations. A luxurious outdoor living area and infinity-edge pool frame panoramic views of a pristine bass lake below — a setting that is spectacular every hour of the day.

The recreation building offers a fitness center, spa, sauna, indoor racquetball court and outdoor tennis/basketball court, plus an indoor kitchen and two bedrooms/baths.

The event venue is highly unique. An income-producing asset if desired, it offers approximately 10,000 square feet of impeccably finished space for corporate retreats, lavish weddings and landmark celebrations, with amenities that include a commercial kitchen and multiple restrooms.

More improvements include a three-bedroom cabin alongside one of the lakes, a foreman's house, hay barn, eight-stall equestrian barn and game-processing station with walk-in freezer and outdoor smoker.

Pearl Ranch's gently rolling terrain offers paved and landscaped roads, scenic riding trails, high-fenced and cross-fenced portions, seasonal wetlands and seven bodies of water, including a gleaming 5-acre lake with boat ramp and boathouse. The world-class wildlife includes white-tailed deer, fallow deer, elk, oryx, blackbuck antelope, dove, turkey, duck and hog. Most remarkably, the ranch's proximity to the metroplex means you can be in a blind at first light and back in Dallas or Fort Worth by lunch — a luxury that few properties can offer.

At 520 Orzo Road in Maypearl, Ellis County, Texas, Pearl Ranch is offered for $16,900,000. For complete information, contact Tyler Thomas, TT Ranch Group: 214-396-9692; info@ttranchgroup.com; ttranchgroup.com; briggsfreeman.com.

LINK TO LISTING

https://ttranchgroup.com/property/pearl-ranch-ellis-texas/100451/

LINK TO VIDEO

https://youtu.be/1tplkeWWnIE

MEDIA CONTACT

Maria Cintron, mcintron@briggsfreeman.com, 214-350-0400

data.title: Global Temperature Sensors Market Outlook 2026-2036: AI, EVs, and Industrial IoT Drive USD 12.76 Billion Expansion
data.description:
Precision Sensing Becomes the Backbone of EV Safety, Smart Manufacturing, and Next-Gen Connected Systems

NEWARK, DE / ACCESS Newswire / April 7, 2026 / The global temperature sensors market is entering a precision-driven growth cycle as industries accelerate automation, electrification, and real-time monitoring. The market is projected to grow from USD 8.38 billion in 2026 to USD 12.76 billion by 2036, registering a steady CAGR of 4.3%.

From electric vehicle battery safety to smart factory predictive maintenance, temperature sensors are rapidly evolving from passive components into mission-critical intelligence layers powering next-generation industrial and digital ecosystems.

Get Access of Report Sample: https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/sample/rep-gb-841

Quick Stats: Temperature Sensors Market (2026-2036)

Metric

Details

Market Size (2026)

USD 8.38 Billion

Forecast Value (2036)

USD 12.76 Billion

Growth Rate (CAGR)

4.3%

Core Demand Sectors

EVs, Industrial IoT, Healthcare, Smart Homes

Leading Market Players

Honeywell, ABB, Texas Instruments, Analog Devices

A Structural Shift: From Measurement to Mission-Critical Intelligence

The role of temperature sensors is undergoing a fundamental transformation. Traditionally used for basic monitoring, sensors are now embedded in complex systems where precision directly determines safety, compliance, and performance outcomes.

In electric vehicles, temperature sensors play a critical role in thermal runaway detection, ensuring battery safety and operational reliability. Even small deviations in accuracy can significantly impact system performance and certification standards.

At the same time, industrial facilities are integrating distributed temperature sensing into predictive maintenance frameworks, reducing downtime and improving efficiency across operations.

Demand and Growth Drivers: Where the Market Is Accelerating

1. Electric Vehicle (EV) Expansion

The rapid scale-up of EV production is fueling demand for high-precision sensing solutions used in battery management systems.

2. Industrial IoT & Smart Manufacturing

Temperature sensors are central to Industry 4.0 transformation:

  • Real-time monitoring

  • Predictive maintenance

  • Process optimization

This is increasing the number of sensors deployed per facility, expanding overall market demand.

3. Healthcare & Medical Applications

Growing reliance on temperature monitoring in:

  • Patient care devices

  • Vaccine and pharmaceutical storage

  • Diagnostic systems

is creating steady, high-value demand.

4. Smart Homes & Connected Ecosystems

Rising adoption of smart thermostats, HVAC systems, and home automation is driving high-volume demand for compact, efficient sensors.

5. Regulatory and Safety Compliance

Strict global regulations across industries are making temperature monitoring a mandatory requirement, not just an operational preference.

Product and Technology Landscape

The temperature sensors market spans a wide range of technologies tailored to specific use cases:

  • Thermocouples & Bimetallic Sensors: Lead the market with strong industrial usage

  • Thermistors: High accuracy for automotive and electronics applications

  • Temperature Sensor ICs: Rapid growth due to integration and compact design

  • Infrared Sensors: Increasing demand for non-contact measurement

  • RTDs (Resistance Temperature Detectors): Preferred for precision environments

Connectivity Trends

  • Wired sensors dominate (66%) due to reliability in critical operations

  • Wireless sensors are growing rapidly, supported by IoT adoption and remote monitoring needs

Regional Outlook: Growth Hotspots

  • India (5.6% CAGR): Fastest-growing market driven by electronics manufacturing and smart infrastructure

  • Brazil (5.1% CAGR): Growth supported by industrial and energy sector investments

  • China (4.8% CAGR): Strong demand from EV production and industrial automation

  • USA (4.5% CAGR): High adoption in semiconductor, aerospace, and data center sectors

  • Germany (4.1% CAGR): Industry 4.0 and sustainability initiatives driving demand

Asia-Pacific continues to dominate global growth, supported by manufacturing expansion and technology adoption.

Competitive Landscape: Innovation as the Core Strategy

The market is moderately consolidated, with leading players competing on precision, reliability, and system integration capabilities.

Key companies include:

  • Honeywell International Inc.

  • ABB Ltd.

  • Analog Devices Inc.

  • Texas Instruments Inc.

  • Robert Bosch GmbH

  • NXP Semiconductors N.V.

Industry leaders are focusing on:

  • Sensor miniaturization

  • AI-enabled sensing systems

  • Multi-function sensor integration

  • Strategic partnerships and acquisitions

Key Takeaways

  • Temperature sensors are becoming critical enablers of automation and digital transformation

  • EVs and Industry 4.0 are the primary long-term growth engines

  • Wireless and smart sensors represent the fastest-growing segments

  • Regulatory compliance is turning sensor deployment into mandatory investment cycles

  • Emerging markets present high-growth opportunities with strong volume demand

Future Outlook: The Invisible Backbone of Smart Systems

By 2036, temperature sensors will evolve into embedded intelligence layers across industries, from mobility and manufacturing to healthcare and energy systems.

As the global economy moves toward automation, sustainability, and precision engineering, temperature sensing will play a silent yet indispensable role-powering safer operations, smarter decisions, and more efficient systems worldwide.

For an in-depth analysis of evolving formulation trends and to access the complete strategic outlook for the Temperature Sensors Market through 2036, Full Report Request: https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/temperature-sensors-market

About Future Market Insights (FMI)

Future Market Insights (FMI) is a leading provider of market intelligence and consulting services, serving clients in over 150 countries. Headquartered in Delaware, USA, with a global delivery center in India and offices in the UK and UAE, FMI delivers actionable insights to businesses across industries including automotive, technology, consumer products, manufacturing, energy, and chemicals.

An ESOMAR-certified research organization, FMI provides custom and syndicated market reports and consulting services, supporting both Fortune 1,000 companies and SMEs. Its team of 300+ experienced analysts ensures credible, data-driven insights to help clients navigate global markets and identify growth opportunities.

data.title: Enverus ONE® is Live - The Governed AI Platform Built for the Energy Industry
data.description:
Enverus ONE® is Live - The Governed AI Platform Built for the Energy Industry

Enverus ONE® is Live - The Governed AI Platform Built for the Energy Industry

PR Newswire

AUSTIN, Texas, April 7, 2026

Purpose-built AI, execution at scale, and decades of proprietary energy data in a single platform

AUSTIN, Texas, April 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Today, Enverus launched Enverus ONE®, the governed AI platform that serves as the new execution layer for the energy industry. Built on decades of data, intelligence and workflows across the energy sector, Enverus ONE accelerates the work of energy by embedding into existing processes, automating tasks, and surfacing answers in minutes that used to take days.

The highest-value work in energy has always been fragmented across data, documents, models, systems, and teams. That fragmentation slows decisions, obscures risk, and traps critical work inside manual loops. Enverus ONE was built to end that.

"Enverus ONE is live," said Manuj Nikhanj, CEO of Enverus. "We have spent decades building the intelligence layer of the energy industry. Enverus ONE is what we have always been building toward: a single, governed platform where fragmented energy work resolves into execution. This is the platform energy runs on. The gap between the companies that move now and the companies that wait is going to be significant and it is going to compound."

While generic AI can reason across the surface, it lacks the operating context that determines how energy assets are evaluated, developed, operated, transacted, and optimized. Enverus ONE is the only AI platform that can, and it gets sharper over time.

Enverus ONE pairs frontier AI models with Astra, Enverus' proprietary energy model. Frontier models provide general intelligence. Astra provides the operating context required to evaluate assets, validate costs, interpret contracts, and resolve energy workflows that generic AI cannot efficiently execute with reliability. Built on the same proprietary data foundation trusted by more than 8,000 energy companies, Astra gives Enverus ONE domain precision that deepens as new Flows, applications and customer work accumulate across the platform.

Enverus ONE resolves work from across upstream, midstream, power, renewables, capital markets, utilities, and adjacent energy infrastructure into auditable, decision-ready work products.

The platform is the foundation. The Flows are the proof.
Enverus ONE launches with four execution-ready Flows designed to eliminate the manual, fragmented processes that slow land, operations, and development teams. Many more Flows are in production and slated for future releases.

  • AFE Evaluation: AFE Evaluation ingests AFEs, validates ownership, and runs offset-based economics automatically compressing evaluation cycles that typically take weeks into hours so teams can determine whether to participate, non-consent, or market their interest with greater speed and defensibility.
  • Current Production Valuation: Current Production Valuation automates the sequence of well selection, production data loading, forecasting, and economic analysis into one connected workflow, reducing manual error and compressing the path from evaluation to action.
  • Project Siting: Project Siting enables developers and analysts to apply multi-criteria filters across land parcels and generate structured multi-site reports, compressing weeks of evaluation across competition, infrastructure access, land availability, grid constraints, permitting realities, and other critical variables into a disciplined workflow.
  • QuickStart: QuickStart allows teams to upload internal documents and immediately stand up a secure knowledge layer for secure conversational Q&A. Instead of losing time across PDFs, presentations, spreadsheets, reports, and internal files, users can begin working against proprietary information from day one.

These Flows are only the beginning. New Flows and capabilities are being added continuously across land, operations, engineering, finance, development, and commercial decision-making — expanding the platform across the energy enterprise.

Work products with enterprise-grade security. Not just chat.
Enverus ONE is built for the real requirements of the energy enterprise. Governance is embedded in the platform. Proprietary customer data remains isolated within a private tenancy, protected by role-based access controls and audit trails, and has zero disclosure to public AI models or third-party ecosystems.

"For energy companies, the barrier to AI adoption is control," said Jimmy Fortuna, Chief Product Officer at Enverus. "Sensitive operational, commercial, technical, and financial data cannot be pushed into loose AI systems and public models. Enverus ONE was built so every interaction is governed, auditable, and contained within a SOC 2 Type II certified private environment. That is what it takes to responsibly move from experimentation to execution at scale."

Enverus has spent over 25 years earning the data, workflows, and trust that make Enverus ONE possible. More than 8,000 energy companies across 50 countries already run on Enverus solutions — inside secure environments that keep proprietary data exactly where it belongs. That is not a starting point. That is an ending point for anyone trying to catch up.

Availability

  • Enverus ONE is available now. New Flows and capabilities will continue to roll into the platform throughout 2026 and beyond.
  • Register for an Enverus ONE webinar featuring live Flow demonstrations on April 15 at 11:00 a.m. CST.

About Enverus
Enverus is the energy industry's AI and data platform, serving more than 8,000 energy companies across 50 countries. Built on 25+ years of proprietary intelligence — 2.7 petabytes of continuously updated data, 350 million+ courthouse records, and $500 billion+ in annual transactions covering the full energy value chain across upstream, midstream, power, renewables, utilities, and capital markets. Enverus is 100% dedicated to energy. Learn more at Enverus.com.

On April 7, 2026, at 1:00 p.m. MST, Enverus will host a press‑only briefing with CEO Manuj Nikhanj, featuring a live demonstration of Enverus ONE and a moderated Q&A for journalists covering energy, technology and markets.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/enverus-one-is-live---the-governed-ai-platform-built-for-the-energy-industry-302735842.html

SOURCE Enverus

data.title: Businesses are spending for the future despite uncertain times - a good omen for the economy
data.description:
By Jeffry Bartash

Companies are investing for the future, but the future is now

Businesses are pouring more money into robotics and artificial intelligence.

The numbers: Business investment rose in March for the seventh time in eight months and hit an all-time high as companies poured more money into the technologies of the future.

The fresh wave of spending on robotics, artificial intelligence and more powerful computers is sowing the seeds for stronger U.S. growth if the Iran war is resolved soon, but the conflict is a drag on the economy in the short run.

So-called core orders for durable goods rose 0.6% in February, pushing the year-over-year increase up to 5.1%.

After declining in 2024 and 2023, investment began to surge at the start of 2024 - and it hasn't let up.

Core orders give a more accurate view of overall business investment. They omit airplanes, cars and military spending because of how lumpy those categories are month to month.

Key details: The investment figures were included in the government's monthly report on durable goods. These are products - such as cars, computers and tools - that are meant to last a long time.

Orders for durable goods dropped1.4% in the month, entirely due to a large decline in new contracts for Boeing (BA) passenger planes.

Aircraft take a few years to build, and contracts are signed at certain times of the year. Economists like to strip out aircraft and vehicles to get a more precise view of industry trends.

The trend is strong. Orders minus transportation rose a healthy 0.8% in February and increased for the 11th straight month.

Big picture: The uncertainty stemming from the Iran war and the legal status of the Trump replacement tariffs hasn't stopped businesses from sinking tons of money into new technologies that promise to reshape the economy.

Business investment is poised to keep rising in the immediate future and to help keep the economy from stalling, although the Iran war could bring a temporary dip.

Looking ahead: "All we can say right now is the trends were positive pre-conflict," Nationwide financial economist Oren Klatchkin said in a research note to clients.

"Positive momentum across durable goods in February should hopefully help this area of the economy persevere through the current turmoil without too much damage," he wrote.

Market reaction: The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA and S&P 500 SPX were set to fall in Tuesday trading on anxiety over the Iran war.

-Jeffry Bartash

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  04-07-26 0947ET

data.title: CPSC Urges Families to Protect Children During National Window Safety Week
data.description:
CPSC Urges Families to Protect Children During National Window Safety Week

CPSC Urges Families to Protect Children During National Window Safety Week

PR Newswire

WASHINGTON, April 7, 2026

WASHINGTON, April 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- As warmer weather arrives, the risk of children falling from open windows increases significantly. In recognition of National Window Safety Week 2026, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) is reminding American parents and caregivers of simple, effective steps to protect children from preventable falls from windows.  

Each year, thousands of young children in the U.S. fall from windows, resulting in serious injury and, in some cases, death. According to CPSC data, an estimated 5,600 children aged 12 and under were treated in emergency departments in 2024 after falling out of a window, and about one in three of those cases required hospitalization. CPSC is also aware of at least 25 deaths among children age 12 and under due to falls from windows between 2021 and 2023. Eighty percent of those fatalities occurred between May and October, the warmest months of the year.

"National Window Safety Week is an important reminder that protecting children starts at home," said CPSC Acting Chairman Peter A. Feldman. "These heartbreaking incidents can happen quickly and quietly and often when least expected. I encourage parents and caregivers to keep simple steps, like installing window guards and locating furniture away from windows, in mind to prevent fall injuries and tragedies."

CPSC recommends families follow these important safety tips:

  • Install window guards to prevent children from falling out of windows.
  • For windows on the 6th floor and below, install window guards that adults and older children can open easily in case of fire.
  • Renters should talk with their landlords about installing window guards.
  • Install window stops so that windows open no more than four inches.
  • Never depend on screens to keep children from falling out of windows.
  • Whenever possible, open windows from the top, instead of the bottom.
  • Keep furniture away from windows to discourage children from climbing near windows.

For more safety tips to help prevent falls from windows, visit CPSC.gov.

About the U.S. CPSC
The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) is charged with protecting the public from unreasonable risk of injury associated with the use of thousands of types of consumer products. Deaths, injuries, and property damage from consumer product-related incidents cost the nation more than $1 trillion annually. Since the CPSC was established more than 50 years ago, it has worked to ensure the safety of consumer products, which has contributed to a decline in injuries associated with these products.

Federal law prohibits any person from selling products subject to a Commission ordered recall or a voluntary recall undertaken in consultation with the CPSC.

For lifesaving information:
- Visit CPSC.gov.
- Sign up to receive our e-mail alerts.
- Follow us on Facebook, Instagram, X, BlueSky, Threads, LinkedIn and Truth Social.
- Report a dangerous product or a product-related injury on www.SaferProducts.gov.
- Call CPSC's Hotline at 800-638-2772 (TTY 800-638-8270).
- Contact a media specialist.

Release Number: 26-384

 

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cpsc-urges-families-to-protect-children-during-national-window-safety-week-302735835.html

SOURCE U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission

data.title: Data Center World 2026 Expo Hall Features World’s Largest Showcase of Cutting-Edge Technologies Driving AI-Powered Data Center Era
data.description:
Data Center World 2026 Expo Hall Features World’s Largest Showcase of Cutting-Edge Technologies Driving AI-Powered Data Center Era

Exhibition Hall Boasts 500+ Exhibitors on April 20-23

Data Center World announced an expanded Expo Hall showcasing 500+ global exhibitors and a wide range of solutions designed to help data center professionals meet rising demands for power, cooling, AI infrastructure, cybersecurity, networking and energy efficiency. No other industry event supports this breadth of industry innovation and scale.

Held April 20-23 at the Walter E. Washington Convention Center, Data Center World 2026 delivers an Expo Hall designed to offer hands-on demonstrations, solution spotlights and direct access to leading providers across the data center ecosystem, giving attendees the opportunity to compare offerings and connect with experts in one place.

“Data Center World’s Expo Hall continues to grow as the industry’s most comprehensive marketplace for data center solutions,” said Tara Gibb, Senior Director, Data Center World. “This year’s expanded floor will give attendees even more opportunities to discover innovations, evaluate technologies and build the partnerships that drive industry operations forward.”

Data Center World 2026 Exhibitor News

  • AIRSYS Cooling Technologies, Booth #1016: Airsys is proud to sponsor Data Center World 2026, showcasing AI-ready thermal solutions. Highlights include UniCool, FluidCool-X and patented LiquidRack spray cooling. Airsys will also feature our PowerOne solution, providing high-efficiency thermal management for next-generation, mission-critical infrastructure.
  • AKCP, Booth #1068: AKCP are pleased to be exhibiting our Environmental Monitoring and DCIM solutions at DCW Washington. Drop by to see how Artificial Intelligence integrated with DCIM can save you energy, help manage assets and improve data center operational efficiency.
  • AMPACE, Booth #206: Ampace introduces the PU200, a semi-solid battery for UPS applications. Delivering 462kW power and 56.52kWh energy with modular configuration, it ensures reliable backup for mission-critical operations. Certified to UL1973 and more, it offers 15-year design life and scalable capacity up to 9 racks. Visit Booth 206 at DCW 2026.
  • AmpLink Tech Corp. Booth #1420: AmpLink is a data center infrastructure solutions provider. Its portfolio includes standard and heavy-duty server racks with shock-resistant packaging, intelligent rack PDUs, rack/row-level CDUs, and manifold cooling solutions to meet growing high-density power demands.
  • Applied High Voltage, LLC, Booth #623: Applied High Voltage (AHV), a high-voltage EPC firm with decades of team continuity, delivers in-house engineering, design and construction for substations, transmission lines and utility switchyards. Supporting the growing data center industry with reliable power infrastructure, Applied High Voltage invites attendees to connect and learn more at Booth 623.
  • BAC, Booth #220: BAC’s COBALT immersion cooling system delivers unparalleled energy efficiency and optimized power use for leading data centers utilizing higher server densities. By combining industry-leading outdoor heat rejection equipment with immersion cooling tanks, we support your advanced and evolving computing needs while enabling sustainable data center operations with a PUE <1.05.
  • Brinkmann Pumps, Inc., Booth #117: Brinkmann Pumps brings more than 75 years of expertise in demanding cooling applications to its AI-ready pump portfolio. Engineered for high-performance CDUs, our pumps integrate smart monitoring and delta p regulation to maximize efficiency and uptime. Our solutions are next-gen TDP ready and provide maximum reliability through advanced materials and maintenance free designs.
  • CargoSense, Booth #1171: CargoSense automates Exception Management for data center construction, ensuring critical shipments arrive on time. Our BUILD software creates real-time supply chain digital twins, using intelligent agents to detect and resolve logistics disruptions instantly. With integrated QR sensor technology, we eliminate costly delays, manual workloads, and keep mission-critical infrastructure on schedule.
  • Chatsworth Products, Booth #409: Chatsworth Products (CPI) will showcase the new VersaEdgeô Wall-Mount Cabinet at Data Center World in Booth 409. Engineered for secure, space-constrained edge deployments, VersaEdgeô delivers deployment-ready scalability, integrated cable management and optimized airflow in a compact footprint.
  • Coilmaster, Booth #1436: Coilmaster will exhibit at Booth 1436 at Data Center World 2026, showcasing precision-engineered heat transfer solutions for data center cooling. With more than 25 years of American manufacturing expertise, Coilmaster helps operators, engineers, and OEM partners meet demanding thermal challenges with performance, reliability, service and short lead times.
  • Concentric, Booth #1424: Concentric is one of the world’s leading pump, fan and thermal management solution manufacturers. We develop innovative technologies and customized solutions which are tailored to the needs of data center customers. Our products include water pumps, oil pumps, fuel pumps, fans, thermal management solutions and hydraulic systems.
  • Controlled Contamination Services, Booth #533: Controlled Contamination Services is the leading contamination control partner for mission-critical data environments. With over 30 years of experience and 62 million square feet of hyperscale and enterprise data centers served, we help facilities teams eliminate contamination risk, protect uptime, and maintain compliance before problems start.
  • CoolIT Systems, Booth #1009: Explore next-generation direct liquid cooling for AI infrastructure at CoolIT Systems, Booth 1009. See advanced coldplates, coldplate loops, and the CHx2000, the world's highest-density 2MW CDU, designed to power the most demanding accelerated compute deployments. Book a meeting: https://www.coolitsystems.com/contact/
  • D Volt Co., Booth #114: D Volt Co. delivers critical power infrastructure solutions designed to compress energization timelines for AI-scale deployments. With diversified manufacturing capacity, U.S.-based engineering, and localized FAT, SAT and service support, D Volt helps developers reduce schedule volatility and protect project returns in today's high-demand power environment.
  • Delta Electronics, Booth #212: Delta will showcase integrated power, cooling, and intelligent building solutions for next-generation AI data centers. From grid-level power delivery to chip-level cooling and facility automation, Delta’s technologies help operators improve efficiency, simplify infrastructure management, and support high-density AI workloads. Visit Delta at Booth 212.
  • Digital Shovel, Booth #612: Digital Shovel’s world premiere of its all new Modular AI Pods, reducing time-to-token from months to days. The launch marks a strategic expansion into AI for Digital Shovel, with innovative pricing starting at $2MM/M, for a North American owned and manufactured product.
  • DP World, Booth #120: DP World. Change what's possible.
  • Eaton Corporation, Booth #850: Eaton is an intelligent power management company that provides hardware, software and services to help you design and build at scale, while operating more sustainable, efficient and economical data centers. We deliver modular data center solutions spanning power, cooling and software that accelerate deployment, ensure reliability and support scalable, sustainable, future-ready operations.
  • Excellerate Products, Booth #1220: Excellerate Products, a division of Faith Technologies Incorporated, is debuting its product OEM brand with a portfolio of pre-engineered, pre-configured power solutions. Featuring UL 891 LV switchboards, the offering delivers faster deployment, scalable performance, and engineered certainty to support today’s AI-driven, mission-critical infrastructure demands.
  • Factory Mutual Insurance Company (FM), Booth #1223: Visit FM is one of the world’s largest commercial property insurers. Visit Booth 1223 to see how FM Intellium protects the data ecosystem from construction through operations. Our engineering-led solutions reduce vulnerabilities across data centers, cooling and power infrastructure to strengthen resilience, insurability and continuity for mission critical digital operations worldwide every day.
  • Forgent, Booth #816: Forgent is a leading U.S. designer and manufacturer of electrical distribution equipment used in data centers, the power grid and energy-intensive industrial facilities. The company specializes in manufacturing custom products that are engineered-to-order for technically demanding applications. We believe Forgent is one of a small number of companies that can manufacture all of the electrical distribution equipment required for a data center or large manufacturing facility's powertrain with some of the highest levels of customization and shortest lead times available in the industry.
  • Greenchip eWaste and ITAD Solutions, Booth #1112: Greenchip is a trusted nationwide partner for decommissioned data center equipment and secure data destruction. We maximize asset value while ensuring strict data security, environmental compliance and transparent reporting. Our turnkey solutions support enterprise, colocation and hyperscale facilities, reducing risk and advancing ESG goals throughout the IT asset end-of-life cycle.
  • Guangxi Yuchai Marine and Genset Power Co., Ltd.. Booth #465: As a global leader in the power industry, Guangxi Yuchai is showcasing advanced diesel engines and generating sets at Data Center World 2026, providing professional and efficient power solutions for data centers. Welcome to Booth 465 for business discussions.
  • Hubbell Inc. Booth #802: Modern data centers require more power than ever. Hubbell provides unified solutions from the grid to the chip, with critical components and modular design across the power train to meet your needs today and grow with you as they evolve.
  • IET Systems, Inc. Booth #427: IET Systems specializes in designing and integrating high-security perimeter protection for data centers and critical infrastructure. From crash-rated barriers and fencing to automated gates and access control, IET delivers complete systems, not just components. Their guiding principle: bring a systems integrator into the design phase early to prevent costly delays and redesign later.
  • IMI, Booth #723: AI makes data center cooling unpredictable, but legacy systems were built for steady loads. Cooling must be designed for dynamic behavior: reacting instantly, safeguarding fluid quality, and locking hydronic stability. IMI delivers a complete, future-ready portfolio from digital control to automatic mechanical solutions, for retrofit and new builds: powering resilient, scalable, energy-efficient data centers with confidence.
  • Industrial Info Resources, Booth #847: Industrial Info Resources is the leading trusted data source for industrial and energy market intelligence. Currently tracking more than $4.1 trillion in global data center projects, IIR delivers the industry’s most comprehensive data center intelligence, providing actionable insights that help businesses identify opportunities, optimize operations and forecast spending trends with confidence.
  • Johnson Controls, Booth #420: Johnson Controls is a trusted leader in the data center industry, helping operators navigate rapid technology shifts, global supply challenges and complex local requirements. With an integrated portfolio spanning thermal management, controls, security and fire systems, Johnson Controls accelerates deployment, improves efficiency and supports reliable operations, backed by scalable manufacturing and global-to-local service expertise.
  • LG Electronics, Booth #416: Optimized Cooling Solution for AI Data Centers.
  • LS Cable and System, Booth #412: LS Cable & System is a global provider of advanced power and communication infrastructure. The company delivers high-capacity cables, busway systems and superconducting solutions supporting next-generation energy and AI data centers. With operations across 19 countries and strategic investments in Virginia, LS Cable & System is expanding its U.S. manufacturing and supply network.
  • LS ELECTRIC, Booth #616: LS ELECTRIC is a global provider of power and automation solutions, driven by our mission Futuring Smart Energy. We deliver safe, efficient total power solutions across the energy value chain, including smart AC & DC UL solutions and advanced energy management from components to switchgear.
  • Maddox Industrial Transformer, Booth #837: Maddox offers a full range of transformers for commercial and industrial use. With thousands of units in stock, our transformers are ready to ship quickly. If we don’t have it in stock, we'll build it - with the shortest lead-times around. We also offer transformer rentals, repairs and surplus transformer purchasing.
  • MiTAC Computing, Booth #210: MiTAC Computing provides server platforms for AI, HPC, cloud, and edge computing designed to support a wide range of enterprise and industry workloads by integrating hardware and software for deployment in data center environments.
  • Modius, Inc. Booth #625: Stop by the Modius booth at Data Center World 2026 to master your infrastructure. Experience OpenDataTM, our vendor-agnostic DCIM platform. See how real-time data unifies power, cooling, and IT assets into a 'single pane of glass' to eliminate silos, boost efficiency, and future-proof your high-density data center for AI.
  • NIMBUS Advanced Process Cooling, Booth #347: Built for the AI era, NIMBUS delivers next-generation heat rejection with dry and hybrid adiabatic cooling. Using 95% less water than cooling towers and 50% less energy than chillers, NIMBUS systems help data centers accelerate permitting, reduce infrastructure risk, and maximize power availability for revenue-generating computers.
  • nVent, Booth #806: We’re excited to announce that the nVent team will showcase their expertise at Data Center World 2026. Join us for engaging discussions, product demos, and insights into the latest trends in the data center industry. Stop by our Tech Talk on Tuesday, April 21 at 1:00pm.
  • OceanTech, Booth #663: Explore the future of hyperscale data center decommissioning with OceanTech at Data Center World 2026. Visit Booth 663 to discuss how AI accelerates IT Asset Disposition, modern decommissioning practices, and why proactive IT end-of-life planning is critical for large-scale operations.
  • Procore Technologies, Booth #1027: Procore Technologies, Inc. is a leading technology partner for every stage of construction. Built for the industry, Procore’s platform drives efficiency and mitigates risk through AI and data-driven insights. Over three million projects run on Procore across 150+ countries.
  • Rexel, Inc. Booth #1209: Visit Rexel in Booth 1209 to explore the epicenter of our comprehensive data center approach that brings unmatched scope and speed to market. We work closely with customers to deliver complete, end-to-end solutions and products.
  • Schneider Electric North America, Booth #602: Schneider Electric announces the availability of the NetShelter Power Shelf, delivering up to 33kW per shelf at 48V for NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 systems. The power distribution platform integrates with our NetShelter Open Architecture racks and installs within EcoStruxure Pod Solutions for AI, providing reliable, scalable power and infrastructure for next-generation accelerated computing.
  • SPOC Energy, Booth #1771: SPOC has rebranded as SPOC Energy, reflecting its expansion beyond oilfield automation into a global power conversion platform supporting critical infrastructure. The company's technologies now serve industries including utilities, data centers, marine systems and industrial power applications, with more than 80,000 systems deployed worldwide supporting reliability across modern energy infrastructure.
  • StarTech.com, Booth #472: StarTech.com brings smarter data center operations to Data Center World. Visit our booth to see our comprehensive portfolio of vending-machine-optimized tools, fiber optic adapters, cleaning supplies, ESD equipment and testers that help teams prevent costly downtime. Explore ESG-friendly packaging and planogram solutions designed to keep critical infrastructure running smoothly.
  • Trane, Booth #650: Explore Trane’s innovations at Data Center World, including co-designed systems and reference designs, our data center virtual environment, liquid cooling and CRAH solutions, new product displays and service capabilities in commissioning, proactive maintenance, and energy optimization. Visit Booth 650 to connect and discover what's next to enable scale and speed to market.
  • TURBINE-X Energy Inc. Booth #1662: Grid instability causes costly downtime and sleepless nights. TURBINE-X Energy provides scalable, vendor-neutral modular power for 1.21 gigawatts and beyond. Request a custom quote today to review your tailored plan and deploy a reliable solution. Don't be left in the dark. Move from cornered to confident with TURBINE-X.
  • VILPE USA, Booth #1331: Protect data center uptime with 24/7 roof monitoring - VILPE Sense provides continuous, IoT-based roof monitoring for data centers. It detects rising humidity levels inside roof structures and sends real-time alerts - replacing the need for routine manual inspections. Address issues early, prevent leaks and protect critical infrastructure from costly downtime.
  • Voltaris Power, Booth #1870: Voltaris Power designs and delivers data center power systems built for reliability, scalability, and uptime. Their switchgear, integrated power centers, unit substations and other custom solutions provide power that performs, built at scale for mission-critical facilities. Voltaris Power delivers infrastructure that doesn’t blink.
  • WTG Energy: Since 1976, WTG Energy has provided reliable, full-lifecycle natural gas pipeline solutions for every mile, from residential and commercial customers to data centers, across Texas, Oklahoma, and beyond. Join our Solutions Spotlight on April 23 from 11:50 a.m. and 12:10 p.m. Visit us in Meeting Room 302 the same day.
  • Zonit, Booth #1118: Zonit is a dynamic product development company that is revolutionizing data center operations through state-of-the-art solutions in, at and to the rack. Zonit boasts an extensive IP portfolio of disruptive products to meet power management challenges of today's datacenters. We displace the status quo and maximize uptime, solving challenges of mission critical environments.

To register for passes to the show, visit www.datacenterworld.com. Interested media can register for a Press Pass here.

Additionally, Data Center World Power (Dallas, September 21-23) and Data Center World Europe (Vienna, October 13-14) speaker submissions are now open. Submit your proposal here. The deadline to apply is April 10.

ABOUT DATA CENTER WORLD

Data Center World is the leading global conference for data center facilities and IT professionals, delivering premier education, expansive networking, and the widest access to best-in-class vendors. For more than 45 years, the event has helped data center and IT infrastructure leaders navigate an increasingly complex and AI-driven landscape. The event provides top-quality, vendor-neutral insights that empower professionals to solve real-world challenges. CIO Dive named Data Center World one of the top technology conferences to attend in 2024 for the second consecutive year. Additionally, for the third year in a row, Data Center World has been recognized by Trade Show Executive as a fast-growing trade show.

Data Center World 2026 will be held April 20–23 in Washington, D.C., uniting thousands of senior practitioners and hundreds of solution providers to explore the future of AI-ready infrastructure, sustainable power, edge and hyperscale innovation, and next-generation facilities.

Data Center World continues to expand its global footprint. To access the Data Center World Global Event series, visit Data Center World's Global Events.

Data Center World is produced by Informa PLC in partnership with AFCOM.

ABOUT AFCOM

AFCOM advances data center and IT professionals by delivering comprehensive and vendor-neutral education and peer-to-peer networking to its members around the world. Through forward-looking research, AFCOM supports thousands of IT, operations, facilities, builders/designers and vendors working in the data center and IT infrastructure industry. In serving the data center industry for more than 40 years, AFCOM has earned recognition as the market leader in educating, connecting and guiding the data center community.

Media Contact: Suzanne Matulay, Society32 for Data Center World, dcwpr@society32.com

data.title: Tyler Technologies Announces 2026 Public Sector Excellence Award Winners
data.description:
Tyler Technologies Announces 2026 Public Sector Excellence Award Winners

Thirty-five winners and honorable mentions recognized at Tyler Connect user conference

Tyler Technologies, Inc. (NYSE: TYL) is proud to announce its 2026 Public Sector Excellence Award winners. Each of this year’s 35 winners and honorable mentions were recognized for their leadership, innovation, and excellence in developing, deploying, or maintaining Tyler solutions within their jurisdictions.

“This year’s excellence award winners have set a new standard for innovation and impact in the public sector,” said Lynn Moore, president and chief executive officer for Tyler. “Across jurisdictions of every size in our total client base of 15,000 locations, these winning organizations are using technology to drive meaningful change, improve efficiency, and better serve their communities. Their achievements demonstrate what’s possible when forward-thinking leadership and modern solutions come together to transform government.”

Tyler’s excellence awards were announced at this year’s Tyler Connect user conference, happening now through April 10 in Las Vegas, Nevada. Nearly 6,000 public sector leaders are attending the conference, which features nearly 800 education and training sessions. Held annually, Tyler Connect features inspiring keynote speakers, sessions covering the latest public sector technology trends, networking opportunities for clients, and hands-on product training, allowing clients to earn continuing education credits.

These jurisdictions were recognized for their significant commitment to public service and striving for greater efficiency and effectiveness in serving their communities. Winners were evaluated and selected by a diverse panel of Tyler leadership in one of the following categories. More information about each winning jurisdiction can be found here.

Civic Interaction & Public Trust

  • City of Lowell, Massachusetts

Connected Community

  • Caddo Parish, Sheriff’s Office, Louisiana
  • City of Homestead, Florida
  • Hidalgo County, Texas
  • City of Pharr, Texas (honorable mention)

Data & Transparency

  • City of Longmont, Colorado
  • Vermont Judiciary
  • Wyoming Highway Patrol

Digital Services & Cloud

  • City of Clemson, South Carolina
  • City of Redondo Beach, California
  • Clackamas Education Service District, Oregon
  • Fort Bend Central Appraisal District, Texas
  • Hidalgo County, Texas
  • Peoria County, Illinois
  • Pinellas County, Florida
  • Village of Glenview, Illinois

Operational Efficiency

  • City of Jacksonville, Texas
  • City of Melbourne, Florida
  • Dallas County, Texas
  • Franklin County Auditor’s Office, Ohio
  • Fulton County Clerk of Superior and Magistrate Courts, Georgia
  • Justice Court, Precinct One of Gillespie County, Texas
  • Medford Municipal Court, Oregon
  • New Hanover County, North Carolina
  • Niles Township School Treasurer, Illinois
  • Ridgeland Police Department, Mississippi
  • Joshua Basin Water District, California (honorable mention)

Performance & Innovation

  • Allentown School District, Pennsylvania
  • City of Buckeye, Arizona
  • City of Liberty Lake, Washington
  • City of Victoria Municipal Court, Texas
  • Lake City, Florida
  • Placer County Probation, California
  • Town of Scarborough, Maine Public Safety
  • Yavapai County, Arizona

The Tyler Public Sector Excellence Awards were established in 2010. Since then, Tyler has awarded more than 300 public sector organizations for excellence in developing, deploying, and maintaining Tyler’s solutions in innovative ways.

About Tyler Technologies, Inc.

Tyler Technologies (NYSE: TYL) is a leading provider of integrated software and technology services for the public sector. Tyler’s end-to-end solutions empower local, state, and federal government entities to operate efficiently and transparently with residents and each other. By connecting data and processes across disparate systems, Tyler’s solutions transform how clients turn actionable insights into opportunities and solutions for their communities. Tyler has more than 45,000 successful installations across 13,000 locations, with clients in all 50 states, Canada, the Caribbean, Australia, and other international locations. Tyler has been recognized numerous times for growth and innovation, including on Government Technology’s GovTech 100 list. More information about Tyler Technologies, an S&P 500 company headquartered in Plano, Texas, can be found at tylertech.com.

#TYL_General

Jennifer Kepler
Tyler Technologies
972.713.3770
Media.team@tylertech.com

data.title: Businesses are spending for the future despite uncertain times - a good omen for the economy.
data.description:
By Jeffry Bartash

Companies are investing for the future, but the future is now

Robots operate on a production line. Businesses are pouring more money into robotics and artificial intelligence.

The numbers: Business investment rose in March for the seventh time in eight months and hit an all-time high as companies poured more money into the technologies of the future.

The fresh wave of spending on robotics, artificial intelligence and more powerful computers are laying the seeds for stronger U.S. growth if the Iran war is resolved soon, but the conflict is a drag on the economy in the short run.

So-called core orders for durable goods rose 0.6% in February, pushing the year-over-year increase up to 5.1%.

After declining in 2024 and 2023, investment begin to surge at the start of 2024 - and it hasn't let up.

Core orders give a more accurate view of overall business investment. They omit airplanes, autos and military spending because of how lumpy they are month to month.

Key details: The investment figures were included in the government's monthly report on durable goods. These are products meant to last a long time, such as cars, computers and tools.

Orders for durable goods dropped1.4% in the month, entirely due to a large decline in new contracts for Boeing (BA) passenger planes.

Aircraft take a few years to build and contracts are signed at certain times of the year more than others. Economists like to strip out aircraft and autos to get a more precise view of industry trends.

The trend is strong. Orders minus transportation rose a healthy 0.8% in February and increased for the 11th straight month.

Big picture: The uncertainty stemming from the Iran war and the legal status of the Trump replacement tariffs hasn't stopped businesses from sinking tons of money into new technologies that promise to reshape the economy.

Business investment is poised to keep rising in the immediate future and help keep the economy from stalling, though the Iran war could bring a temporary dip.

Market reaction: The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA and S&P 500 SPX were set to fall in Tuesday trading on anxiety over the Iran war.

-Jeffry Bartash

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  04-07-26 0917ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
